holdings.Name: Oil And Natural Gas Corporation Ltd.

exit_load: Exit load of 1% if redeemed within 1 year.
tax_implication: If you redeem within one year, returns are taxed at 20%. If you redeem after one year, returns exceeding Rs 1.25 lakh in a financial year are taxed at 12.5%.

about_fund.description: Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by Quant Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 14 Apr 1996. Sanjeev Sharma is the Current Fund Manager of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Manageme Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by Quant Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 14 Apr 1996. Sanjeev Sharma is the Current Fund Manager of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹88,796 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 18 Mar 2026 is ₹38.21. The Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹1,000. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹5,000. Exit load of 0.50% if redeemed within 3 months. ...Read more
about_fund.investment_objective: The fund aims to invest predominantly in equity and equity related instruments of the companies in the infrastructure sector.

fund_house.address: 6th Floor, Sea Breeze Building,Appa Saheb Marathe Marg,Prabhadevi, Mumbai 400025

faqs.question: How to Invest in Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: You can easily invest in Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth in a hassle-free manner on Groww. The process is extremely simple, quick and completely paperless. Invest in a few minutes with the following steps:Log on to your Groww accountSearch for Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth from the search boxIn order to invest, you will have to complete all the KYC formalities which are completely online and paperless and take a few minutes to completeOnce you are done with that, you can start investing in Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth as SIP or lumpsum as per your investment objective and risk tolerance

faqs.question: What kind of returns does Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth provide?
faqs.answer: The Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth has been there from 07 Jan 2013 and the average annual returns provided by this fund is 3.62% since its inception.

faqs.question: How much expense ratio is charged by Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: The termExpense Ratioused for Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth or any other mutual fund is the annual charges one needs to pay to the Mutual Fund company for managing your investments in that fund.The Expense Ratio of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is 0.88% as of 19 Mar 2026...

faqs.question: What is the AUM of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: The AUM, short forAssets Under Managementof Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is ₹2,869.01Cr as of 19 Mar 2026.

faqs.question: How to Redeem Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: If you want to sell your Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth holdings, go to your holding on the app or web and simply click on it. You will get two options - redeem & invest more; click on redeem and enter your desired amount or if you wish to redeem the entire holding amount then select the 'redeem all' checkbox.

faqs.question: Can I invest in SIP and Lump Sum of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: You can select eitherSIPorLumpsuminvestment of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth based on your investment objective and risk tolerance.

faqs.question: What is the NAV of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: The NAV of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is ₹38.21 as of 18 Mar 2026.

faqs.question: What is the PE and PB ratio of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth?
faqs.answer: ThePE ratioratio of Quant Infrastructure Fund Direct Growth is determined by dividing the market price by its earnings per share and thePB ratioof the same is evaluated by dividing the stock price per share by its book value per share (BVPS).
